title: Home Purchase/Construction Financing/Refinancing - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 42 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ALBANY AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. PO Box 2400 Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -Are United States citizens or permanent residents and residents of the Greater Albany School District for the past year -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history, able to manage current debt) Substandard housing may include poor heating or wiring, a leaky roof, mold, not having enough space for your family, or paying 50% or more of your income towards housing. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon Email (541) 967-4030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEND-REDMOND HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with the opportunity to build and purchase their own homes. Homes are sold to qualified families using below-market-rate financing. Participating families contribute volunteer hours and engage in the community as part of the program. 224 NE Thurston Avenue Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing that meet all of the following requirements: -Live or work in Bend or Redmond for at least 1 year -Meet income guidelines listed on website -Commit to community engagement and volunteering -Primary applicant is a United States citizen or permanent resident -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to make a mortgage payment (steady income) -Have a minimum credit score of 620 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 385-5387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Homeownership program sells Habitat constructed houses at affordable cost based on income to individuals unable to afford a typical mortgage. Program works directly with homeowners during construction to complete general homeownership and financial education. 4840 SW Philomath Boulevard Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Family selection factors include demonstrated need for housing (present housing is inadequate), ability to pay monthly mortgage, income between 35%-80% of the county's median income based on family size, and willingness to partner. Must live or work in Benton County. Hours: Home construction: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Office: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 752-6637 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF SPRINGFIELD SPRINGFIELD HOME OWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a home ownership program to assist low and moderate income residents with the first-time purchase of a home. The city can loan up to $25,000 toward the down payment and related costs of buying a qualified home. The loan is interest-free, and repayment is generally not required until the home is sold, refinanced, or transferred. Loan availability is dependent on funding. 225 5th Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: -Be a first-time home buyer or have not owned a home in the past three years -Meet income requirements -Be prequalified for a home loan -Successfully completed the Homebuying Foundations class through DevNW -Contribute a minimum of 50% of the minimum down payment required by the primary lender Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA COUNTY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families, who provide sweat equity labor to help build the houses. 164 Little Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing that meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 400 hours of sweat equity in construction -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to make a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (971) 203-0020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA GORGE HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are built with volunteer labor and donated materials and are then sold at no profit to homeowner families. Agency does not provide immediate or emergency housing. This is a long-term housing program. 111 E 5th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing and/or in need of safe and affordable housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 350 hours of sweat equity in construction -United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to make a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) -Must credit qualify and pass background checks -Must already reside in the service area Hours: Tuesday/Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 296-8817 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about mortgage resources, down payment assistance programs, foreclosure prevention, and default counseling. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE DOWN PAYMENT LOAN ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides first-time homebuyers with up to $15,000 for down payment or closing cost assistance. The loan is forgivable after five years if the buyer remains in the home. If the buyer moves out before then, they must repay a prorated portion of the loan. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Must be a low to moderate income registered tribal member and must be listed on the deed of the house Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 888-9577 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS COUNTY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOME OWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families, who provide sweat equity labor to help build the houses. 760 S 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Family currently living in substandard housing who: 1) meet income guidelines 2) can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction 3) is a United States citizen or permanent legal resident 4) Have not owned a home in the past 3 years 5) has ability to pay a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: Monday/Friday by appointment only, Tuesday-Thursday 10am-4pm Email (541) 756-1600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW CORVALLIS DOWN PAYMENT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a combination of education, counseling, and down payment financial assistance for income-qualified first-time homebuyers to cover a portion of the down payment and/or closing costs associated with the purchase of a home (when funding is available). 2525 SE Third Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: -Low to moderate income households -Be a first time homebuyer; cannot have owned a home in the past three years -Purchase a single-family home, including a detached home, a condo, or a manufactured home on owned land -Occupy the home -Additional loan requirements may apply Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (541) 752-7220 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW OREGON CITY DOWN PAYMENT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a combination of education, counseling, and down payment financial assistance for income-qualified first-time homebuyers to cover a portion of the down payment and/or closing costs associated with the purchase of a home (when funding is available). 421 High Street Suite 110 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: -Low to moderate income households -Be a first time homebuyer; cannot have owned a home in the past three years -Purchase a single-family home, including a detached home, a condo, or a manufactured home on owned land -Occupy the home -Additional loan requirements may apply Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (503) 655-8974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW SALEM DOWN PAYMENT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a combination of education, counseling, and down payment financial assistance for income-qualified first-time homebuyers to cover a portion of the down payment and/or closing costs associated with the purchase of a home (when funding is available). 528 Cottage Street NE Suite 1C Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Low to moderate income households -Be a first time homebuyer; cannot have owned a home in the past three years -Purchase a single-family home, including a detached home, a condo, or a manufactured home on owned land -Occupy the home -Additional loan requirements may apply Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (503) 779-2680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW SPRINGFIELD DOWN PAYMENT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a combination of education, counseling, and down payment financial assistance for income-qualified first-time homebuyers to cover a portion of the down payment and/or closing costs associated with the purchase of a home (when funding is available). 212 Main Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: -Low to moderate income households -Be a first time homebuyer; cannot have owned a home in the past three years -Purchase a single-family home, including a detached home, a condo, or a manufactured home on owned land -Occupy the home -Additional loan requirements may apply Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (541) 345-7106 2053 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FLORENCE HABITAT FOR HUMANITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to build and buy homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. 2004 Highway 101 Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-4pm Email (541) 902-9227 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANTS PASS AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. Mortgage payments go into a revolving fund that is used to build more houses. Homeowner families and volunteers provide most of the labor, and individual and corporate donors provide money and materials to build Habitat houses. 409 NE E Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 150 hours of sweat equity per adult over 18 years of age, with a family maximum of 500 hours -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) -Have the ability to be responsible for maintenance and repairs on a new home after move in -Have the ability to complete financial counseling and household maintenance education -Must live in Josephine County and be willing to live in Grants Pass Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon. Closed federal holidays. Email (541) 474-2884 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HABITAT FOR HUMANITY LA PINE SUNRIVER OWN YOUR OWN HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. 56835 Venture Lane Suite 101-102 Sunriver, OR 97707 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 400 hours of sweat equity by volunteering on new home construction, volunteering to help at the ReStore in La Pine, or volunteering to help with another activity approved by Habitat -United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 593-5005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HABITAT FOR HUMANITY OF CENTRAL LANE HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for people to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner partners, who provide participation hours in construction and other opportunities to help build the houses. 1210 Oak Patch Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing that meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines (people within the 40-80% area median income - see website for full income requirements) -Can commit to 200-250 hours of participation hours including construction (100 hours) and 100+ hours of financial courses, support meetings, and other volunteer opportunities -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to make a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: All services are by appointment only: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 741-1707 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HABITAT FOR HUMANITY OF LINCOLN COUNTY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. Houses are built with volunteer labor and use donated materials. Volunteer opportunities available. 1255 NW US 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Households with income between 45-70% of area median income, based on family size; must have demonstrated need; must be willing to partner and complete sweat equity. Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (458) 277-7601 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HABITAT FOR HUMANITY OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY HOMEOWNERSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are built with volunteer labor and donated materials and are then sold at no profit to homeowner families. Agency does not provide immediate or emergency housing. This is a long-term housing program. 1220 12th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Households that meet all of the following requirements: -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Must live in affiliate service area for at least 12 months -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have not had a bankruptcy discharged within the past 3 years -Are currently living in substandard, subsidized housing, overcrowded or unaffordable housing -Can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -Must have steady income sufficient to repay an affordable mortgage and satisfactory credit history -Household within 50% to 80% of the Marion County median household income Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday by appointment. Closed public holidays. Email (503) 364-6642 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HABITAT FOR HUMANITY PORTLAND REGION HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families and individuals to build and purchase their homes with affordable mortgages. Eligibility: Households currently living in substandard housing that meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 300 hours of sweat equity in construction -Are US citizens or permanent residents -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to make a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 287-9529 1324 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HACIENDA COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers bilingual homeownership counseling for first-time homebuyers, as well as those facing foreclosure. Also offers a HUD-certified homebuying course and access to down payment assistance options when available. 6700 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 961-6432 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) rental assistance, including a Homeownership Voucher Program. Also offers a Family Self-Sufficiency program, Housing Rehab program, and manages several area affordable housing complexes for families, farmworkers, older adults, and people with special needs. 135 NE Dunn Place Mcminnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Varies; some programs may have residency or income requirements. Call for information. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 883-4300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEBANON AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families, who provide sweat equity labor to help build the houses. 566 S Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Applicants must: meet income requirements, be in need of affordable housing, willing to partner with Habitat, and be able to make mortgage payments. Hours: Varies Email (541) 405-5213 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MCMINNVILLE AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. Houses are built with volunteer labor and use donated materials, when available. Mortgage payments received from homeowners are recycled into a fund to build more homes in the McMinnville area. This is a long term housing solution. 1040 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Total annual income of 40%-65% of the Area Median Income (AMI) for Yamhill County -Can commit to 200-400 hours of sweat equity in construction and other partnership opportunities, working alongside volunteers -United States citizens or permanent residents with one year of residency or work in the service area -Have not had a bankruptcy in at least 4 years -A decent credit history and criminal background check. Emphasis is on most recent history and is evaluated based on individual circumstances (e.g., no minimum credit score). Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 472-9637 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA AND COLUMBIA GORGE HOUSING AUTHORITIES MID-COLUMBIA HOUSING RESOURCE CENTER HOUSING RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides education, information, and referral on housing issues, along with financial assistance for homebuyers and homeowners. Services include homebuyer education classes and workshops, financial education, pre- and post-purchase counseling, foreclosure counseling, down payment assistance, renter information, and guidance on maintaining and repairing a home, including home repair loans. Classes are held monthly at Columbia Gorge Community College in Hood River and The Dalles. The program also keeps the public informed about housing trends in the Mid-Columbia region using current statistics. 500 E 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm and by appointment Email (541) 296-3397 18 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT BEND OFFICE HOMESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers first-time home ownership classes, counseling, financial literacy classes, individualized financial coaching, credit counseling, down payment assistance loans, reverse mortgage counseling, mortgage payment assistance, help with property taxes, asset building, matched savings programs known as Individual Development Accounts, and low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 20310 Empire Avenue Suite A100 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT LA PINE OFFICE HOMESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers first-time home ownership classes, counseling, financial literacy classes, individualized financial coaching, credit counseling, down low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 51530 Huntington Road Suite 6 La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get programs known as Individual Development Accounts, and low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 374 SW 5th Street Suite B Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT PRINEVILLE OFFICE HOMESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers first-time home ownership classes, counseling, financial literacy classes, individualized financial coaching, credit counseling, down payment assistance loans, reverse mortgage counseling, mortgage payment assistance, help with property taxes, asset building, matched savings programs known as Individual Development Accounts, and low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 457 NE Ochoco Plaza Drive Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 323-0394 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT REDMOND OFFICE HOMESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers first-time home ownership classes, counseling, financial literacy classes, individualized financial coaching, credit counseling, down payment assistance loans, reverse mortgage counseling, mortgage payment assistance, help with property taxes, asset building, matched savings programs known as Individual Development Accounts, and low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 2303 SW 1st Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT WARM SPRINGS OFFICE HOMESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers first-time home ownership classes, counseling, financial literacy classes, individualized financial coaching, credit counseling, down payment assistance loans, reverse mortgage counseling, mortgage payment assistance, help with property taxes, asset building, matched savings programs known as Individual Development Accounts, and low-interest, payment-deferred home rehabilitation loans. 1144 Warm Springs Street Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 553-0497 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEWBERG AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY NEWBERG AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity houses are built with volunteer labor and use many donated materials. Families contribute 500 hours (minimum) of "sweat equity" working alongside volunteers during construction of their homes. After a house is complete, it is sold to the family at an affordable base price. Mortgage payments received from homeowners are recycled into a Fund for Humanity to build more homes in the area. 620 N Morton Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 537-9938 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTH WILLAMETTE VALLEY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at an affordable price to homeowners. From application to owning a home takes approximately 18-24 months. Habitat is a long-term homeownership program and does not provide emergency housing. 225 Franklin Street Mount Angel, OR 97362 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Income guidelines -Can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not have owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment with steady income and decent credit history Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 845-2434 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOMEOWNER ASSISTANCE AND RECONSTRUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance to homeowners whose properties were damaged or destroyed by wildfires and/or straight-line winds in 2020. Assistance may include: -Repairing an originally damaged or replacement stick-built or manufactured home -Building a new stick-built home on owned or leased land -Replacing a damaged manufactured home on land a homeowner owns, leases, or will secure a lease for -Purchasing a stick-built or manufactured home that is already installed Cannot reimburse any money a homeowner has already spent to repair or restore their home. New owners of a home damaged in the disaster are not eligible for HARP assistance. Cannot assist with second homes, foreclosed homes, recreational vehicles, campers, detached structures, housing units that are not permitted, mixed-use buildings, and multiplexes with five or more units. Eligibility: -Homeowners whose home was damaged or destroyed due to wildfires and/or straight-line winds in 2020 -Have remaining construction repairs of at least $3,000 -Home must be primary residence -Must be in good standing or payment plan with mortgage and property taxes Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 250-0938 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOUSING SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with rent and utility assistance (electric, gas, heating fuel, sewer, trash, or water), down payment for a home, or financial counseling, which may include household budgeting, eviction protection, and legal aid. Rent assistance participants must pay 30% of their income towards rent. Assistance is available for 24 months or until the program’s funding is entirely spent. Eligibility: -Household affected by the wildfires and straight-line winds of 2020, which include the 242, Almeda, Archie Creek, Beachie Creek, Echo Mountain, Holiday Farm, Lionshead, Riverside, and South Obenchain fires -At the time of the disasters, the applicant lived in an impacted county -Current household income at or below 120% of Area Medium Income (AMI) -Rent/Utility Assistance: Monthly housing costs exceed 30% of applicant's household’s adjusted gross monthly income or housing is not decent, safe, or sanitary and the cost of housing that meets occupancy standards would exceed 30% of household's income Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 250-0938 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND HOUSING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Resource center for homebuyers, providing pre-purchase counseling, homeownership education, individual development accounts, down payment assistance, and financing. Does not provide any type of rental assistance or construction assistance. Mortgage Assistance Program provides down payment assistance and financing. Does not help with reverse mortgages or individuals experiencing or at risk of foreclosure. 3233 NE Sandy Boulevard Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted for HomeOwner Basics program. Must be at or below 80%-100% Median Family Income (MFI) for mortgage assistance and down payment assistance. Hours: By appointment only. Evening and weekend appointments and classes available. Email (503) 282-7744 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROUD GROUND COMMUNITY LAND TRUST HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home ownership opportunities. Types of properties that are eligiblie include condos, single family homes, remodeled homes, and new construction. 541 NE 20th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Households with income between 40%-80% of the Area Median Income (AMI); participating households typically earn $3,500-$6,500 per month before taxes. Limited opportunities for households earning up to 100% AMI. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 493-0293 112 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. 2201 S Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Meet income guidelines -Can commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -Are United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to pay a mortgage payment (steady income and decent credit history) Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-1983 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SISTERS HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. A "Notification List Request" is available for applications which does NOT provide preference or any other advantage in applying for the homeownership program. 221 S Ash Street Sisters, OR 97759 Eligibility: Families currently living in substandard housing who meet all of the following requirements: -Income guidelines -Can commit to partnership equity (working toward ownership in a multitude of ways) -United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -The ability to pay a mortgage payment; steady income and decent credit history Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 549-1193 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK COUNTY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HOMEOWNERSHIP/PARTNER FAMILY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. 4192 Highway 101 N Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following requirements: -Income guidelines (chart on website) -Commit to 500 hours of sweat equity in construction -United States citizens or permanent residents -Have not owned a home in the past 3 years -Have the ability to commit to a mortgage payment, which may require a steady income and credit history to be vetted by the organization Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 842-7472 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE RURAL DEVELOPMENT OREGON STATE SECTION 502 DIRECT HOME LOAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Section 502 Direct Home Loan Program provides loans in eligible rural areas for low- and very-low-income households to purchase, build, or repair their existing home. Applicants may obtain 100 percent financing to buy an existing dwelling, purchase a site and have a dwelling constructed, or purchase a newly built home. Qualifying applicants receive a low-interest, fixed-rate home loan from USDA for up to 38 years with no money down. 1220 SW 3rd Avenue Suite 1801 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: -Live in a rural area -Be without decent, safe and stable housing -Unable to obtain a loan from other resources on terms and conditions that can reasonably be expected to meet -Agree to occupy the property the primary residence -Have the legal capacity to incur a loan obligation -Meet citizenship or eligible non-citizen requirements -Not be suspended or debarred from participation in federal programs -Meet income eligibility for a direct loan Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (866) 923-5626 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT PORTLAND FIELD OFFICE DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides referrals and information about income-based housing through local Public Housing Authorities and other providers, Federal Housing Administration (FHA) loans, manufactured housing; and referrals to HUD-approved housing counseling agencies for foreclosure, purchase, reverse mortgage, and budgeting assistance. A list of HUD-funded housing is available online. 1220 SW 3rd Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 972042825 Eligibility: Income restrictions vary by program. Acceptance of rental application is at apartment manager's discretion. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 222-2600 0 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WEST TUALITY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for families to build and buy their own homes. The houses are sold at no profit to homeowner families. 3731 Pacific Avenue Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following requirements: 1) Has a demonstrated housing need 2) Can commit to 250 hours per adult of sweat equity in construction 3) Are United States citizens or permanent residents 4) Have not owned a home in the past 3 years 5) Have the ability to pay a mortgage
